prevalence.trend: decreasing long-term but plateau in some groups

incidence.source: School health surveys and tobacco surveillance

burden.dalyPerCapita: high; major risk factor for CVD, COPD and cancer

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Self-reported tobacco use; assess dependence with standardized questionnaires (e.g., Fagerstrom)

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions of current or former smokers
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; cessation success improves with counseling and pharmacotherapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care screening and brief intervention programs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Behavioral counseling, nicotine replacement, bupropion or varenicline where available; quitline support
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Relapse, youth vaping uptake, and uneven access to cessation programs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong tobacco control policies and established cessation programs
